Biology on track

Full-time
4 terms

T1 BIOLOGY 3
Part-time
—

Supply diagnostics (2 terms)

Rotation gaps

Required courses offered in too few of the terms you checked. A chair can read this as students may have to wait for the next rotation.

  • none

Single-section risk

Only one section is available in the analyzed window. One cancellation, room problem, or time conflict can block the path.

  • none

Capacity / fill-rate

Capacity / fill-rate needs real Cap/Tot enrollment counts. On live-only builds, an empty result usually means not yet measurable, not all clear.

  • none

Under-supply

Shows waitlist pressure. With live data it counts waitlisted sections; with an enrollment export it can show exact waitlist headcount. Treat it as a planning signal, not proof of completion impact.

  • none

Time conflicts

Required courses meet at overlapping times, so a student cannot take the planned combination without delaying something.

  • none

Off-grid sections

Sections starting outside the standard time-block grid. Off-grid starts can create avoidable conflicts; the broad campus grid-pressure view stays in the Grid conformance panel.

  • none

Program buildability

Not computed: no program / sections to audit

Fewest offering changes to buildable

Contact-hour conformance

Not computed: no section carries units + weeks-of-instruction + a meeting time

... contact-hour CONFORMANCE proxy ...

Why this matters — research evidence

Sector-wide research evidence — published findings from OTHER institutions that explain WHY a flagged structural condition plausibly matters. This is NOT a measurement of this campus and NOT a prediction of this campus's outcomes.

No structural flags fired — general guided-pathways context only.

  • 30%→43%; 9%→~18%; 23.7%→34.8% — Guided-pathway course maps with structured, data-informed advising raised graduation rates: Central Arizona College 30%→43%, Community College of Philadelphia 9%→~18% (doubled), and Bluegrass / BCTC 23.7%→34.8%.Source: Central Arizona College, Community College of Philadelphia, Bluegrass Community & Technical College; reported via Community College Daily and CCRC
  • Odessa: +12pp retention / +13% credentials; Kilgore: 26%→33% — Eight-week terms and standardized scheduling blocks improved outcomes: Odessa College in-class retention +12 percentage points and credentials awarded +13%; Kilgore College 150% graduation rate 26%→33%.Source: Odessa College, Kilgore College; reported via Community College Daily
Generated by SchedulePlanner. Live data is from LACCD’s public class-schedule API and Program Mapper; the analysis is section- and aggregate-level only (no student-level records). Prerequisite / GE coverage is a planning aid — confirm with a counselor.
